summary: Iteration 6 (frontend-only) — Validated the 4 new features on preview URL: (1) Case-study media stacked vertically with first full-width and second ~60% (Bracket, Verizon Tello, Hyundai×B&O, One Audio all verified w1=747px, w2=448px → ratio 60%). (2) Product Designer chip computed styles: bg=rgb(10,7,4) near-black ✓, text=rgb(245,165,36) amber ✓, dot=rgb(245,165,36) amber ✓. (3) Behind the Pixels CMD box computed style: bg=rgba(0,0,0,0.55) translucent ✓, box-shadow contains 4 amber layers (245,165,36) ✓, backdrop-filter=blur(6px) ✓. (4) Peek mode: '/' opens overlay ✓, input auto-focused ✓, typing 'figma' → workbench-chip-figma gets peek-match while cursor/linear/notion/framer/miro/origami get peek-dim ✓, typing 'ship' → principle-01 (Ship the smallest thing) peek-match and other principles peek-dim ✓, typing 'audio' in case studies → harman-kardon (One Audio) peek-match ✓ and Hyundai×B&O ALSO peek-match (because its searchable text includes 'audio' via tags/subtitle — semantically correct but stricter test criterion partially unmet), Escape closes overlay AND clears all peek-match/peek-dim classes ✓, PEEK MODE hint text present ✓, StatusBar '/' + Peek label present ✓. Regressions: about-me-avatar loads, 2 bracket-link anchors on About Me + 1 on Bracket case study all → https://use-bracket.com/, open-designs-bracket only on Bracket, Cmd/Ctrl+K palette opens correctly. 21/22 automated assertions pass; the 1 nuanced miss ('Hyundai×B&O also matching audio') is expected data behavior, not a defect.
action_items: OPTIONAL: The peek 'audio' query in case studies also highlights 'Hyundai × Bang & Olufsen' (in addition to 'One Audio'). This is because CaseStudyListItem's searchText concatenates title+category+role+tags+subtitle, and Hyundai×B&O's data legitimately contains 'audio'-related terms. If the spec strictly wants only exact title-based matching, narrow the search corpus to `c.title` only; otherwise leave as-is (semantically the current behavior is correct — B&O is an audio brand).

OPTIONAL (testability): Add data-testid="command-palette" and role="dialog" to /app/frontend/src/components/CommandPalette.jsx root for easier automated verification. Functionally the palette works via Cmd/Ctrl+K.
critical_code_review_comments: Peek '/' handler correctly guards against being triggered when INPUT/TEXTAREA is focused and against modifier keys — clean implementation.
usePeekTarget memoizes on [open, query, text] — good perf.
PeekSearch overlay uses pointer-events none on backdrop with a full-cover pointer-events-auto close button behind — good UX for click-outside close.
BehindThePixels.jsx CMD box style is inline; box-shadow multi-layer amber (0.18 ring, 0.35 drop, 0.15 glow, 0.06 inset) renders correctly.
Work.jsx figure stacking uses flex-col with first `w-full` and subsequent `w-full md:w-3/4 lg:w-2/3 xl:w-3/5 self-start` — verified w2/w1 ≈ 0.60 at 1920 viewport.
AboutMe Product Designer chip uses inline style background:#0a0704 and color:var(--amber) — meets contrast/spec exactly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 new features (media stacking, Product Designer chip, translucent CMD box with amber shadow + backdrop-blur, peek mode with '/' shortcut) are working correctly on preview URL. The only nuance is that peek 'audio' matches both One Audio and Hyundai×B&O in the case-study list — this is because the searchable text concatenates title+category+role+tags+subtitle, and Hyundai×B&O's dataset legitimately contains audio-related tokens. If reviewer wants stricter matching, narrow the corpus. Case study IDs used in mock data: 'bracket', 'verizon-tello', 'hyundai-bo', 'harman-kardon' (NOT 'tello-ai' or 'one-audio' as mentioned in some review notes).
